---
title: "Teaching & Outreach"
---

## Teaching

**International Economics** — *2026*
École de Management Léonard de Vinci (EMLV), Bachelor in International Business — 15 h

**Environmental Economics and Biodiversity Valuation** — *February 2023*
AgroParisTech – Université Paris-Saclay, Master in Economics and Public Policy — 6 h

**Integrated Assessment of Ecosystem Services** — *December 2022 & December 2023*
AgroParisTech – Université Paris-Saclay, Master in Agricultural Engineering — 6 h

**Supervision of a Master's Thesis** — *2024*
Paris School of Economics (PSE), Master in Public Policy and Development (PPD)
*"The tree that hides the forest? An exploratory approach to evaluating trade-offs in ecosystem restoration projects. A case study on the Great Green Wall in Senegal."*
Joint supervision with François Libois (PSE) and Antoine Godin (AFD).

**Ecosystem Accounting** — *December 2022 & December 2023*
Paris School of Economics (PSE), Bachelor in Environmental and Resource Economics — 6 h

**Macroeconomics** — *September – December 2022*
Université Paris-Dauphine – PSL, Bachelor in Economics — 64 h

---

## Seminar Organisation

**Philosophy of Ecological Inequalities** — *2025–2026*
Monthly seminar series, co-organizer — hosted at École Normale Supérieure (ENS), Paris

**Ecological Economics** — *2025–2026*
Monthly seminar series, co-organizer — hosted at EHESS, Paris

---

## Selected Presentations

### International Conferences

- **2025** — "Chronicle of a Debt Foretold: Sovereign Debt Management Strategies for Colombia's Climate and Biodiversity Transition," *BIOECON Conference*, Cambridge, UK
- **2025** — "Selective Carbon Credits: Market Preferences and Ecosystem Restoration in Senegal," *LAERE*, Bogotá, Colombia
- **2024** — "Selective Carbon Credits: Ecosystem Restoration and Market Preferences," *BioEcon*, Cambridge, UK
- **2024** — "North-South Financial Flows for Biodiversity: A Remedy or an Aggravation of Hierarchical Patterns?" *Global Goals Conference*, Utrecht, Netherlands
- **2024** — "Building a Multidimensional Biodiversity Harmful Subsidies Indicator," *FAERE*, Paris, France
- **2022** — "Biodiversity Harmful Subsidies," *15th Conference on Strong Sustainability*, AFD, Paris, France

### Invited Talks & Seminars

- **January 2026** — "Building Green Debt Strategies in a Fossil Fuel-Dependent Economy," *CEEM Seminar*, Montpellier
- **December 2025** — "Construire des stratégies nationales de financement de la biodiversité," *UMI Source Academic Seminar*, Guyancourt
- **2025** — "Sovereign Debt Management Strategies for Colombia's Climate and Biodiversity Transition," *Workshop on Macroeconomic Challenges of the Transition*, Pisa, Italy
- **2025** — "Chronicle of a Debt Foretold," *Oxford–LSE Nature Finance Initiative*, Oxford, UK
- **2025** — "Selective Carbon Credits," *Duke UPEP Seminar*, Durham, USA
- **2025** — "Debt Relief for Colombia's Climate and Biodiversity Goals," *EAERE Winter School*, Annecy, France
- **2024** — "Selective Carbon Credits," *CIRED Seminar*, Paris
- **2024** — "Contribution of the Voluntary Carbon Market to Land Restoration in Senegal," *PPSEE*, Paris

### National Conferences

- **2025** — "'Restorative Economics': A Research Agenda for Economic Studies in the Context of Environmental Justice," *Political Economy Association*, Bordeaux, France
- **2023** — "Financing Ecological Restoration: The Case of Senegal," *Geographic Bioeconomy: Water and Energy*, Université de La Rochelle, France

---

## Contact

**Email:** morgane.gonon [at] agroparistech.fr

**Address:**
CIRED — Centre International de Recherche sur l'Environnement et le Développement
45 bis avenue de la Belle Gabrielle, 94130 Nogent-sur-Marne, France

**Profiles:**
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=PE5P9CoAAAAJ) &nbsp;|&nbsp;
[ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0004-4811-4442) &nbsp;|&nbsp;
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/morgane-gonon-861879106/) &nbsp;|&nbsp;
[GitHub](https://github.com/MGononDufour) &nbsp;|&nbsp;
[CIRED](https://www.centre-cired.fr/en/)
